Name: Vercel - Deploy
Description:
Triggers a git-connected deployment of a [Vercel](https://vercel.com) project via the [Vercel REST API](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api), and optionally waits for the deployment to finish building. Supports projects connected to GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket repositories.

The Vercel project must be connected to the repository via Vercel's git integration (this also covers private repositories); the step never accesses the repository directly, so no git credentials are needed in Octopus.

Sets the output variables `DeploymentId`, `DeploymentUrl`, and `DeploymentState` for use in later steps.

Requires `curl` and either `jq` or `python3` on the worker.

Properties.Octopus.Action.Script.ScriptBody:
#!/bin/bash

# Vercel - Deploy
# Triggers a git-connected deployment on Vercel via the REST API, and
# optionally waits for the deployment to finish building.
# API reference: https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/reference/endpoints/deployments/create-a-new-deployment

# Read step parameters
ApiToken=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.ApiToken")
ProjectName=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.ProjectName")
GitProvider=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.GitProvider")
GitRef=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.GitRef")
RepoOrg=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.RepoOrg")
RepoName=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.RepoName")
RepoId=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.RepoId")
Target=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.Target")
TeamId=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.TeamId")
WaitForDeployment=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.WaitForDeployment")
TimeoutSeconds=$(get_octopusvariable "VercelDeploy.TimeoutSeconds")

# Validate required tooling

if ! command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "ERROR: curl is required on the worker to run this step." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  JSON_TOOL="jq"
elif command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  JSON_TOOL="python3"
else
  echo "ERROR: jq or python3 is required on the worker to build and parse JSON." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Prints the JSON-encoded (quoted) form of $1
json_escape() {
  if [[ "$JSON_TOOL" == "jq" ]]; then
    jq -cn --arg v "$1" '$v'
  else
    python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.dumps(sys.argv[1]))' "$1"
  fi
}

# Reads JSON on stdin and prints the value at dotted path $1 (empty if missing or null)
json_get() {
  if [[ "$JSON_TOOL" == "jq" ]]; then
    jq -r ".$1 // empty" 2>/dev/null
  else
    python3 -c '
import json, sys
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
except Exception:
    sys.exit(0)
cur = data
for part in sys.argv[1].split("."):
    if isinstance(cur, dict) and part in cur:
        cur = cur[part]
    else:
        sys.exit(0)
if cur is None:
    sys.exit(0)
print(cur if not isinstance(cur, (dict, list)) else json.dumps(cur))
' "$1"
  fi
}

# Validate required parameters

if [[ -z "$ApiToken" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: ApiToken is required." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [[ -z "$ProjectName" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: ProjectName is required." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [[ -z "$GitProvider" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: GitProvider is required (github, gitlab, bitbucket)." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [[ -z "$GitRef" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: GitRef is required (branch name or commit SHA)." >&2
  exit 1
fi

TimeoutSeconds="${TimeoutSeconds:-600}"
if ! [[ "$TimeoutSeconds" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Timeout must be a whole number of seconds." >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Normalise provider to lowercase
GitProvider=$(echo "$GitProvider" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')

# Build gitSource payload

REF_JSON=$(json_escape "$GitRef")

case "$GitProvider" in
  github)
    if [[ -z "$RepoOrg" || -z "$RepoName" ]]; then
      echo "ERROR: RepoOrg and RepoName are required for GitHub deployments." >&2
      exit 1
    fi
    GIT_SOURCE="{\"type\":\"github\",\"org\":$(json_escape "$RepoOrg"),\"repo\":$(json_escape "$RepoName"),\"ref\":$REF_JSON}"
    ;;
  gitlab)
    if [[ -z "$RepoId" ]]; then
      echo "ERROR: RepoId is required for GitLab deployments." >&2
      exit 1
    fi
    GIT_SOURCE="{\"type\":\"gitlab\",\"projectId\":$(json_escape "$RepoId"),\"ref\":$REF_JSON}"
    ;;
  bitbucket)
    if [[ -z "$RepoOrg" || -z "$RepoName" ]]; then
      echo "ERROR: RepoOrg (workspace) and RepoName (slug) are required for Bitbucket deployments." >&2
      exit 1
    fi
    GIT_SOURCE="{\"type\":\"bitbucket\",\"owner\":$(json_escape "$RepoOrg"),\"slug\":$(json_escape "$RepoName"),\"ref\":$REF_JSON}"
    ;;
  *)
    echo "ERROR: Unsupported GitProvider '$GitProvider'. Must be one of: github, gitlab, bitbucket." >&2
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# Build request body

# Target defaults to "production". Valid values: production, staging, or a custom environment identifier.
DEPLOY_TARGET="${Target:-production}"

BODY="{\"name\":$(json_escape "$ProjectName"),\"gitSource\":$GIT_SOURCE,\"target\":$(json_escape "$DEPLOY_TARGET")}"

# Build URL with optional teamId

API_URL="https://api.vercel.com/v13/deployments"
if [[ -n "$TeamId" ]]; then
  API_URL="${API_URL}?teamId=${TeamId}"
fi

# Make the API request

echo "Triggering Vercel deployment..."
echo "  Project : $ProjectName"
echo "  Provider: $GitProvider"
echo "  Ref     : $GitRef"
echo "  Target  : $DEPLOY_TARGET"
[[ -n "$TeamId" ]] && echo "  Team ID : $TeamId"
echo ""

RAW=$(curl --silent --show-error --max-time 120 --write-out $'\n%{http_code}' \
  --request POST "$API_URL" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $ApiToken" \
  --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data "$BODY")

CURL_EXIT=$?

if [[ $CURL_EXIT -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: curl request failed (exit code $CURL_EXIT)." >&2
  exit 1
fi

HTTP_STATUS=$(echo "$RAW" | tail -n 1)
RESPONSE=$(echo "$RAW" | sed '$d')

# Parse response

ERROR_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | json_get "error.code")
ERROR_MSG=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | json_get "error.message")

if [[ -n "$ERROR_CODE" || "$HTTP_STATUS" -ge 400 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Vercel API returned an error (HTTP $HTTP_STATUS)." >&2
  [[ -n "$ERROR_CODE" ]] && echo "  Code   : $ERROR_CODE" >&2
  [[ -n "$ERROR_MSG" ]] && echo "  Message: $ERROR_MSG" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Extract deployment details

DEPLOY_ID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | json_get "id")
DEPLOY_URL=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | json_get "url")
DEPLOY_STATE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | json_get "readyState")

if [[ -z "$DEPLOY_ID" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Could not read the deployment id from the Vercel API response." >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "Deployment created."
echo "  ID    : $DEPLOY_ID"
echo "  State : $DEPLOY_STATE"
echo "  URL   : https://$DEPLOY_URL"

set_octopusvariable "DeploymentId" "$DEPLOY_ID"
set_octopusvariable "DeploymentUrl" "https://$DEPLOY_URL"
set_octopusvariable "DeploymentState" "$DEPLOY_STATE"

if [[ "$WaitForDeployment" != "True" ]]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Wait for deployment is disabled; not waiting for the build to finish."
  exit 0
fi

# Poll until the deployment is READY, fails, or the timeout is reached

STATUS_URL="https://api.vercel.com/v13/deployments/${DEPLOY_ID}"
if [[ -n "$TeamId" ]]; then
  STATUS_URL="${STATUS_URL}?teamId=${TeamId}"
fi

echo ""
echo "Waiting up to ${TimeoutSeconds}s for the deployment to become READY..."

INTERVAL=10
ELAPSED=0

while true; do
  STATUS_RAW=$(curl --silent --show-error --max-time 60 \
    --header "Authorization: Bearer $ApiToken" \
    "$STATUS_URL")

  if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: Status check failed; retrying..." >&2
  else
    DEPLOY_STATE=$(echo "$STATUS_RAW" | json_get "readyState")
    echo "  [${ELAPSED}s] State: ${DEPLOY_STATE:-UNKNOWN}"

    case "$DEPLOY_STATE" in
      READY)
        set_octopusvariable "DeploymentState" "$DEPLOY_STATE"
        echo ""
        echo "Deployment is READY: https://$DEPLOY_URL"
        exit 0
        ;;
      ERROR|CANCELED)
        set_octopusvariable "DeploymentState" "$DEPLOY_STATE"
        echo "ERROR: Deployment finished in state $DEPLOY_STATE. Check the build logs in the Vercel dashboard." >&2
        exit 1
        ;;
    esac
  fi

  if (( ELAPSED >= TimeoutSeconds )); then
    set_octopusvariable "DeploymentState" "${DEPLOY_STATE:-TIMED_OUT}"
    echo "ERROR: Timed out after ${TimeoutSeconds}s waiting for the deployment to become READY (last state: ${DEPLOY_STATE:-unknown})." >&2
    exit 1
  fi

  sleep "$INTERVAL"
  ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + INTERVAL))
done

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.ApiToken
Parameters.Label: API Token
Parameters.HelpText: A Vercel API token. Create one at [vercel.com/account/tokens](https://vercel.com/account/tokens). Consider binding this to a sensitive project variable (e.g. `#{Vercel.ApiToken}`) so it can be scoped per environment and rotated in one place.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.ProjectName
Parameters.Label: Project Name
Parameters.HelpText: The name of the Vercel project to deploy.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.GitProvider
Parameters.Label: Git Provider
Parameters.HelpText: The git provider the Vercel project is connected to.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.GitRef
Parameters.Label: Git Ref
Parameters.HelpText: The branch name or commit SHA to deploy. For repeatable deployments across environments, bind this to a commit SHA captured at release creation (e.g. from build information or a prompted variable) rather than a branch name, so every environment deploys the same commit.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.RepoOrg
Parameters.Label: Repository Owner
Parameters.HelpText: GitHub org/username or Bitbucket workspace. Not required for GitLab.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.RepoName
Parameters.Label: Repository Name
Parameters.HelpText: The repository name or Bitbucket slug. Not required for GitLab.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.RepoId
Parameters.Label: Repo ID
Parameters.HelpText: GitLab project ID. Only required for GitLab.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.Target
Parameters.Label: Deployment Target
Parameters.HelpText: Valid values: production, staging, or a custom environment identifier.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.TeamId
Parameters.Label: Team ID
Parameters.HelpText: Vercel team ID. Only required for team-owned projects.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.WaitForDeployment
Parameters.Label: Wait For Deployment
Parameters.HelpText: If enabled, the step polls the Vercel API until the deployment reaches the READY state, and fails if the build errors, is canceled, or the timeout is reached.

Parameters.Name: VercelDeploy.TimeoutSeconds
Parameters.Label: Wait Timeout (seconds)
Parameters.HelpText: How long to wait for the deployment to become READY before failing the step. Only used when Wait For Deployment is enabled.
